image_prompts.prompt: Hook visual: Nearby stars shift as Earth moves—this shift reveals their distance.
Context visual: This phenomenon is called stellar parallax. It's all about perspective.
Escalation visual: As Earth orbits the Sun, stars appear to slightly shift against the background.
Evidence visual: Using trigonometry, astronomers calculate distances based on this tiny shift.
Payoff visual: This method allows us to measure stars thousands of light-years away!
Cta visual: Curious about more cosmic mysteries? Like and subscribe for more insights!
